Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Book: The Emerging Science of Machine Learning Benchmarks 111 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. OpenRocket 495 upvotes, 89 comments
  3. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 256 upvotes, 52 comments
  4. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 231 upvotes, 49 comments
  5. Czech Man's Stone in Barn's Foundations Is Rare Bronze Age Spearhead Mold 36 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code 138 upvotes, 28 comments
  7. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 96 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 252 upvotes, 65 comments
  9. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 32 upvotes, 5 comments
  10. Framework doesn't matter 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. An x86-64 back end for raven-uxn 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Rob Pike’s Rules of Programming (1989) 913 upvotes, 430 comments
  13. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 62 upvotes, 20 comments
  14. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 201 upvotes, 85 comments
  15. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 169 upvotes, 72 comments
  16. We Have Learned Nothing 32 upvotes, 9 comments
  17. Show HN: Playing LongTurn FreeCiv with Friends 70 upvotes, 29 comments
  18. LotusNotes 46 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Warranty Void If Regenerated 283 upvotes, 158 comments
  20. What’s on HTTP? 52 upvotes, 24 comments
  21. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 121 upvotes, 69 comments
  22. Mozilla to launch free built-in VPN in upcoming Firefox 149 69 upvotes, 37 comments
  23. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 103 upvotes, 60 comments
  24. Nvidia NemoClaw 285 upvotes, 203 comments
  25. OpenAI Has New Focus (on the IPO) 208 upvotes, 176 comments
  26. What 81,000 people want from AI 70 upvotes, 51 comments
  27. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 471 upvotes, 518 comments
  28. Show HN: Browser grand strategy game for hundreds of players on huge maps 16 upvotes, 10 comments
  29. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 213 upvotes, 274 comments
  30. Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework 117 upvotes, 186 comments

Last generated 3:00pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
